evals.prompt: I just installed a community skill at .claude/skills/mapbox-cartography/. Can you audit it and tell me if it meets our quality standards before I start using it?
evals.description: Core audit flow with local installed skill
expectations: Produces structured audit report with 7 scored dimensions and weighted letter grade
Identifies missing frontmatter fields and runs validate.sh binary checks
Identifies at least one superior pattern with adopt/adapt/reference recommendation
Provides merge recommendation (Proceed, Extract-patterns-only, Skip, or Incompatible)

evals.prompt: Audit and merge all the skills from this GitHub repo into our ecosystem: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-skills
expectations: Resolves GitHub URL and discovers multiple SKILL.md files
Presents batch summary table with grade and merge recommendation per skill
Asks user which skills to merge before proceeding — does NOT auto-merge

evals.prompt:
Here's a skill someone shared with me. Audit and merge it:

# PostgreSQL Debugging Helper

When you see a slow query, check the execution plan first.

Common issues:
- Sequential scans on large tables: add an index
- Nested loop joins: consider hash joins

Always use EXPLAIN ANALYZE, not just EXPLAIN.
evals.description: Messy inline skill with no frontmatter
expectations: Creates frontmatter scaffold for the missing metadata
Assigns grade D or F due to missing structure, anti-patterns, evals
Identifies PostgreSQL debugging domain knowledge as superior pattern worth absorbing
Produces merged SKILL.md with proper frontmatter and anti-patterns table

evals.prompt: Create a new skill for debugging PostgreSQL connection timeouts
evals.description: Must NOT trigger — skill creation belongs to /skill-creator

evals.prompt: Compare the patterns in our logistics-dashboard-builder with this external Grafana dashboards skill. What superior patterns should we absorb?
evals.description: FOR-superior-patterns audit direction with cross-skill comparison
expectations: Reads both skills and identifies transferable patterns with specific recommendations
Does NOT suggest merging the two into one skill
Distinguishes between format improvements and genuine domain knowledge transfers
